compiled_state.title: DSO NextJS Starter: one-command bootstrap for non-engineer prototyping
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
Context: Non-engineering users — designers, PMs, and other non-technical stakeholders — want to use DSO to rapidly prototype client demo ideas. Today, bootstrapping a new DSO-ready project requires multiple manual steps: cloning the plugin, running setup scripts, answering a 7-area onboarding interview, and running additional skills before any feature work begins. This creates a barrier that prevents non-engineers from reaching the 'describe what I want to build' moment without help from an engineer. The DSO NextJS Starter removes that barrier: a single curl command on macOS installs all dependencies, scaffolds a pre-configured Next.js project, and opens Claude Code so the user can immediately type /dso:roadmap. The installer scaffolds the project by cloning a public GitHub template repository (created as part of this epic as a fork of navapbc/template-application-nextjs). Both the template repository and the installer script are deliverables of this epic.

Approach: Option A — Fork navapbc/template-application-nextjs as a public GitHub repo under the DSO org; installer script lives at plugins/dso/scripts/create-dso-app.sh in the DSO plugin repo. Script clones the fork via git clone, performs sed-based project name substitution with shell-metacharacter sanitization, installs npm deps, pre-bakes all DSO infrastructure files, and opens claude. Dependency foundation: Homebrew.

Success Criteria:
1. On a macOS machine with Homebrew already installed but no other project-specific tools (Node, git, pre-commit, Claude Code absent), the installer completes all steps and opens Claude Code within 10 minutes measured from the curl command on a connection capable of >=25 Mbps download; running ls in the opened Claude Code session shows the scaffolded project directory.
2. The installer checks for and installs all required dependencies via Homebrew (bash 4+, git, GNU coreutils, Node 20.x via brew install node@20 with explicit PATH injection, pre-commit, Claude Code via brew install --cask claude-code); for each dependency it cannot install automatically, it prints 'Run: brew install <package>' and exits non-zero with 'Prerequisites not met — re-run after installing the above.'
3. The scaffolded project is a valid Next.js application (contains package.json with next as a dependency, an app/ or pages/ directory, and npm run dev starts without errors) pre-wired with DSO infrastructure files (project-understanding.md, design-notes.md, ARCH_ENFORCEMENT.md, dso-config.conf, CLAUDE.md, .pre-commit-config.yaml, .github/workflows/ci.yml) such that invoking /dso:roadmap in the first Claude Code session produces a roadmap prompt without emitting any gate-blocked error.
4. The installer performs project name substitution throughout all template files; project names containing shell-unsafe characters (spaces, slashes, special regex chars) are sanitized or rejected with an actionable error message.
5. Before the plugin installation step, the installer prints a numbered explanation and pauses for acknowledgment; if the user sends EOF/Ctrl-C, the installer exits with code 1 and prints 'Installation cancelled.'
6. The installer uses a .dso-init-complete sentinel file to distinguish fully initialized from partially initialized projects; running the installer in a fully initialized directory exits informatively; a partially initialized directory prompts the user to resume or start fresh.
7. The installer script lives at plugins/dso/scripts/create-dso-app.sh in the DSO plugin repo and is invocable via curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/<org>/digital-service-orchestra/HEAD/plugins/dso/scripts/create-dso-app.sh | bash.
8. An automated smoke-test script (tests/scripts/test-create-dso-app.sh) runs the installer end-to-end in a sandboxed temporary directory with mocked dependency checks, and asserts the expected project structure, substituted project name, and presence of the .dso-init-complete sentinel; the test asserts exit code 0 on newline acknowledgment and exit code 1 on stdin close.

Priority: Value=4 (Significant — directly enables non-engineer prototyping use case), Effort=3 (Moderate — installer script + template fork + pre-baked artifacts) -> P2

comments.body:
PLANNING_INTELLIGENCE_LOG

=== Brainstorm Session Summary ===
Session date: 2026-04-10
Skill: /dso:brainstorm
Epic: baff-7163

=== Scrutiny Pipeline Results ===
Gap Analysis: No artifact gaps. Part B surfaced YAML injection + file-existence guard risks (carried to scenario analysis). Part C: 5 uncovered design-notes.md consumers (all read-only; write-back fills existing fields without schema changes — no action required).

Web Research: Skipped (no external integration signals for primary epic; feasibility research run separately in brainstorm Phase 1).

Scenario Analysis: 5 survivors (2 filtered)
- Partial download / partial init leaves corrupt project (high, runtime) → mitigated: main() wrapper + .dso-init-complete sentinel
- Node 20.x keg-only PATH not active in same shell session (high, runtime) → mitigated: explicit PATH injection before npm
- tickets orphan branch missing in fresh clone (high, runtime) → mitigated: pre-created with empty commit in template fork
- sed metacharacter corruption in project name (medium, runtime) → mitigated: project name sanitization
- claude inherits piped stdin from curl pipe (high, runtime) → mitigated: exec < /dev/tty before launching claude
Filtered: enabledPlugins silently ignored (addressed by extraKnownMarketplaces approach in spec + spike story in SC3)

Fidelity Review: Round 2 — all dimensions pass
- Agent Clarity: self_contained=4, success_measurable=4
- Scope: right_sized=5, no_overlap=5, dependency_aware=5, consumer_completeness=N/A
- Value: user_impact=4, validation_signal=4
- Feasibility: technical_feasibility=4, integration_risk=3 (moderate; spike story embedded in SC3)
Revision cycles: 1 (Round 1 had 4 failing dimensions)

Prompt Alignment: Skipped (installer script is not LLM-facing instruction)

=== Complexity Classification ===
Tier: COMPLEX
Qualitative overrides: multiple_personas, external_integration, success_criteria_overflow (8 SCs)
Confidence: high
External boundaries: 4 (GitHub, Homebrew, npm, Claude Code CLI)
Pattern familiarity: medium (common ecosystem pattern, novel to repo)
feasibility_review_recommended: true (already completed during brainstorm scrutiny pipeline)

=== Key Technical Decisions ===
- Option A: Fork + git clone + bash substitution (chosen over nava-platform approach due to simplicity for non-engineers)
- Installer script lives in DSO plugin repo at plugins/dso/scripts/create-dso-app.sh
- Roadmap design-intent write-back is a separate follow-on epic (07c2-6ac0)
- plugin install is necessarily interactive (no --non-interactive flag); mitigated by explicit user instructions + extraKnownMarketplaces pre-baking
- Node version managed via explicit PATH injection (not brew link --force which is session-scoped)

=== Routing ===
COMPLEX → /dso:preplanning
Suggested first story: spike for extraKnownMarketplaces + enabledPlugins combined settings.json approach (verify plugin consent flow triggers on first Claude launch)
comments.author: Test

comments.body:
PREPLANNING_CONTEXT

=== Phase 1: Context Reconciliation ===
Escalation Policy: Escalate when blocked
Complexity: COMPLEX (qualitative overrides: multiple_personas, external_integration, success_criteria_overflow)
No scope, feasibility, or clarity conflicts found in pre-existing ticket state.

=== Phase 2: Risk & Scope Scan ===
Risk Register:
- RISK-01 [HIGH/RUNTIME]: curl | bash stdin truncation — exec < /dev/tty mid-script replaces pipe feed stream, may truncate script on some bash versions. Mitigation: canonical invocation bash <(curl -sL url) (process substitution). Addressed in S3 and S5.
- RISK-02 [HIGH/RUNTIME]: tickets orphan branch absent from fresh clone — git clone without --no-single-branch fetches only default branch. Mitigation: --no-single-branch flag added to S3 done definition.
- RISK-03 [HIGH/RUNTIME]: Node 20.x keg-only PATH not propagated to subprocess boundary. Mitigation: explicit PATH injection before exec < /dev/tty in S4 done definition.
- RISK-04 [HIGH/RUNTIME]: dso.plugin_root missing post-clone — all /dso:skill invocations fail. Mitigation: new S7 story covers machine-local path write at install time.
- RISK-05 [MEDIUM/RUNTIME]: sed metacharacter corruption in project name. Mitigation: sanitization logic in S3 done definition.
- RISK-06 [HIGH/RUNTIME]: partial download leaves corrupt project. Mitigation: main() wrapper + .dso-init-complete sentinel in S3.

No split candidates identified (all stories are appropriately scoped at 1–3 days each).

=== Phase 2.5: Adversarial Review ===
Red team (opus): 14 findings across 6 categories
Blue team (sonnet): 9 accepted, 5 rejected
Key accepted findings applied:
- BT-01: Mock claude binary via PATH stub in T1 tests
- RT-01: S1 must document both success AND failure paths (unblocks S2+S5 conditional)
- RT-02: ARCH_ENFORCEMENT.md must be at .claude/ARCH_ENFORCEMENT.md, not bare docs/
- RT-03: git clone --no-single-branch required for tickets orphan branch
- RT-04: Node PATH injection must precede exec < /dev/tty call
- NEW-S7: DSO shim plugin_root write is a critical missing story (most impactful structural gap)

=== Phase 3: Walking Skeleton & Vertical Slicing ===
Walking skeleton critical path: T1 → S2 (unblocked by S1) → S3 → S7 → S5
Parallel track: S4 runs concurrently with S3 (both blocked by T1; S4 not blocked by S2)
Foundation stories: T1, S1, S2, S3, S4, S7 — installer works and scaffold is functional
Enhancement stories: S5 (interactive UX polish), S6 (documentation)

INVEST compliance: all stories pass (Independent, Negotiable, Valuable, Estimable 1–3 days, Small, Testable)

=== Phase 4: Stories Created ===
T1 [1d8e-0a6f] p1 — TDD gate: failing test suite (blocks S3, S4, S5, S7)
S1 [a894-b583] p1 — Spike: plugin consent settings.json (blocks S2)
S2 [caf7-e03d] p1 — Template fork with DSO files (blocked by S1; blocks S3)
S3 [8356-7324] p1 — Core installer: clone + substitution + sentinel (blocked by T1, S2; blocks S7, S5, S6)
S4 [a00b-12fe] p1 — Dependency detection + Node PATH (blocked by T1; blocks S5, S6)
S7 [a70a-a389] p1 — DSO shim: write dso.plugin_root post-clone (blocked by T1, S3; blocks S5)
S5 [fc3f-8832] p2 — Interactive UX + idempotency (blocked by T1, S3, S4, S7; blocks S6)
S6 [9af5-f216] p3 — Documentation update (blocked by S3, S4, S5)

Total: 8 stories | 6 Foundation + 2 Enhancement
